Pass: Alert headline appears 2 or 3 times. Fail: Headline appears only once, as in a press release or news bulletin format.
items.wi_reason: The alert headline '*** CIVIL DANGER WARNING ***' appears 3 times at the top, consistent with EAS triple-header protocol.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills output begins with 'EMERGENCY ALERT SYSTEM MESSAGE' appearing only once. While 'REPEAT' appears at the end, the primary alert headline is not repeated in EAS triple-header format.

items.criterion: The SOURCE section identifies the engineering or regulatory authority that confirmed the failure (e.g., California Department of Water Resources, structural engineers) rather than a weather detection system. The HAZARD section describes the inundation or flood wave threat rather than meteorological phenomena. Pass: SOURCE references an engineering/regulatory body and HAZARD describes flood/inundation. Fail: SOURCE references NWS Doppler radar or weather satellite, or HAZARD section is absent.
items.wi_reason: SOURCE section explicitly states 'ENGINEERS FROM THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF WATER RESOURCES.' HAZARD section describes 'IMMINENT CATASTROPHIC DAM FAILURE WITH RAPID, LIFE-THREATENING FLOODING' — correctly references engineering authority and inundation threat.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills output mentions structural engineers and flood wave in prose but lacks dedicated SOURCE and HAZARD labeled sections. The criteria require identifiable labeled sections, which are absent.

items.criterion: The PRECAUTIONARY/PREPAREDNESS ACTIONS section directs residents to evacuate immediately (not shelter-in-place) and includes at least one specific routing directive (e.g., named highway or direction of travel away from the Feather River corridor). Pass: Evacuation is explicitly directed and at least one named or directional route is provided. Fail: Section advises shelter-in-place, provides only generic 'leave the area' language with no routing specifics, or is absent.

items.criterion: The alert lists the specific downstream communities at risk (Oroville, Thermalito, Gridley, Yuba City, or Marysville) in the affected locations or LOCATIONS IMPACTED section rather than only naming the county or dam location. utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run (using the generate-american-emergency-alert-system-warning skill) produced a fully compliant EAS-formatted alert with all 10 structural sections properly labeled, EAS visual separators (*~*~*), triple header repetition, dedicated SOURCE/HAZARD/IMPACT sections referencing the correct engineering authority, and explicit evacuation routing. It passed all 4 verifier checks. The wo_skills run produced a competent emergency announcement with correct content (named communities, routing, agency attribution) but entirely failed EAS structural compliance: no visual separators (failed verifier V4), no section labels, no triple header repetition. While wo_skills scored on content-based items (J4, J5, J6, J7), it failed all structure-based items (J1, J2, J3). The skill provides critical value in enforcing EAS broadcast format discipline that a baseline agent does not spontaneously apply.
